Yes — lance-datafusion is essential for medcare-rs. Without it, Lance is just a columnar vector store. With it, Lance tables are full DataFusion TableProviders — meaning SQL queries over patient records, joins across entity types, predicate pushdown, and the RLS rewriter all work directly on Lance-native data.

The medcare-rs session should add lance-datafusion = "=4.0.0" alongside lancedb. It comes free transitively through lancedb 0.27.2 (which pins lance-datafusion = =4.0.0), but making it an explicit dep lets medcare-rs use LanceDatafusion::register_dataset() to wire Lance datasets into their SessionContext without going through lancedb's table abstraction.

That's the difference between "store embeddings in Lance" (vector-only) and "query patient records with SQL over Lance" (full relational). The second is what medcare-rs Phase 2 actually needs.

 config change.** Every phase can be reverted to the prior phase in under one hour by toggling a feature flag — no data migration, no schema change.
- **No phase introduces new data loss vectors.** All writes go to MySQL first; Lance is downstream. If Lance loses a write for any reason, MySQL still has it.
- **GDPR compliance is strengthened, not weakened.** The new audit log captures reads (today: not captured); RBAC is enforced at the database level (today: enforced only in the application layer); patient data isolation is per-praxis (today: by row ownership but not at the database level).

## What the practice has to do

- **Phase F1 prep:** approve the dual-write activation. No staff work.
- **Phase F3 cutover:** approve the cutover window. Backups verified beforehand. No staff work during the cutover itself.
- **Phase F4 staff orientation:** ~1 hour group session for clinical staff to understand the new menu items (cohort search, lab-trend, diagnosis suggestion).
- **Phase F5 RBAC review:** sit with IT for ~2 hours to review and confirm role assignments match practice expectations.

Total practice involvement: **~5 hours of staff time across 3–4 months**, plus standard backup-verification and acceptance-test sign-offs.

## Comparison summary

| | Today (C# + MySQL) | Tomorrow (Rust + Lance + Foundry-shape) |
|---|---|---|
| Storage | MySQL only | MySQL (oracle) + Lance (working store) |
| Search | Exact match (name, KdNr) | Exact + similarity + cohort + trend |
| Audit | Writes only, application-layer | Reads + writes, database-layer, signed |
| API surface | None (desktop-only) | REST + WebSocket, standardized |
| Clinical AI | Embedded, opaque | Explicit, explainable, auditable |
| Multi-praxis | Each praxis isolated, no shared learning | Each praxis isolated **plus** opt-in shared learning without shared data |
| Time travel | Daily backups only | Per-second time travel on every record |
| Performance regression | (baseline) | None measured; same or faster on every operation |
| Vendor lock-in | C# / Windows / MySQL | Open-format (Apache Arrow / Lance / DataFusion) |

## Summary in one sentence

MedCare keeps everything you have, adds a learned knowledge graph alongside it, and exposes both through a standard interface — so your practice gets cohort similarity, lab-trend match, and diagnosis suggestion **without abandoning a single record, retraining a single staff member's core workflow, or trusting a single AI decision blindly**.

# Document 2 (revised) — `docs/client/medcare-umstellung.md`

# MedCare — Umstellung auf neue Plattform

## Funktionen — vorher / nachher

| Funktion | Bisher (C# + MySQL) | Neu (Rust + Lance + MySQL) |
|---|---|---|
| Patientensuche | Exakte Suche (Name, KdNr) | Exakt + Ähnlichkeit + Kohorte |
| Laborwerte | Tabelle, manuelle Auswertung | Tabelle + Trendverlauf + Mustervergleich |
| Diagnose-Eingabe | Manuell, ICD-10-Codeliste | Manuell + Vorschlagsliste aus ähnlichen Fällen |
| Audit-Log | Nur Schreibvorgänge, Anwendungsebene | Lese- und Schreibvorgänge, Datenbankebene, signiert |
| Externe Schnittstelle | Keine (nur Desktop) | REST + WebSocket (Supabase-Form) |
| Wartezimmer-Anzeige | Polling | Push in Echtzeit |
| Laborergebnis-Anzeige | Manueller Refresh | Push beim Eintreffen |
| Zeitreise auf Datensatz | Nur über Backup-Restore | Sekundengenau, im laufenden Betrieb |
| Mehrere Praxen | Isoliert, kein Austausch | Isoliert + optional anonymer Wissensaustausch ohne Datenaustausch |
| Berechtigungen (RBAC) | Anwendungsebene | Datenbankebene (Row Level Security) |
| Patientenportal | Nicht vorhanden | Optional aktivierbar |
| Telemedizin-/Drittapp-Anbindung | Custom-Integration nötig | Standard-REST-Anbindung |
| Reporting / Ad-hoc-Auswertung | Nur durch Entwickler | SQL direkt durch IT/Verwaltung |

## Wartung — vorher / nachher

| Aufgabe | Bisher | Neu |
|---|---|---|
| Datenbank-Backup | mysqldump nightly | mysqldump nightly (unverändert) + Lance-Snapshot pro Commit |
| Patch / Update einspielen | Stop Desktop, Patch, Neustart | Hot-Reload Server, Desktop unverändert |
| Wartungsfenster pro Patch | ~30 min Downtime | 0 min |
| Schema-Änderung | ALTER TABLE + Restart | ALTER TABLE (MySQL) + automatische Lance-Migration |
| Rollback einer Version | Backup einspielen, mehrere Stunden | Feature-Flag umlegen, < 1 h |
| Multi-Mandantentrennung | Anwendungslogik (Risiko bei Bug) | Datenbank-RLS (greift unabhängig von App-Bug) |
| Logfiles | Windows-Eventlog | Strukturierte JSON-Logs + getrennter Audit-Trail |
| Performance-Tuning | MySQL-Indizes manuell | MySQL-Indizes + Lance-Statistik automatisch |
| DSGVO Art. 30 (Verzeichnis Verarbeitungstätigkeiten) | Manuelle Pflege | Automatisch aus Audit-Log generierbar |
| Anbindung neuer Apps | Entwicklung pro App | Standard-REST, ohne Code-Änderung an MedCare |

## Was während der Umstellung mit den Daten passiert

| Phase | Dauer | Daten | Was die Praxis merkt |
|---|---|---|---|
| F1 | 2–4 Wochen | Jeder Schreibvorgang geht in MySQL **und** Lance. MySQL bleibt führend. | Nichts. |
| F2 | 4–6 Wochen | Neue REST/WebSocket-Schnittstelle aktiv. Reconciler vergleicht MySQL und Lance laufend, meldet Abweichungen. | Nichts auf dem Desktop. |
| F3 | 2 Wochen | Desktop-App liest über die neue Schnittstelle. MySQL nur noch Referenzsystem. | Nichts (bei Null-Drift). Rollback per Feature-Flag möglich. |
| F4 | 4–6 Wochen | Neue Funktionen (Kohortensuche, Trendvergleich, Diagnosevorschlag, Echtzeit-Wartezimmer) live. | Neue Menüpunkte. |
| F5 | 1 Woche + laufend | RBAC (Arzt / MFA / Admin / Patient) auf DB-Ebene aktiv. Patientenportal optional. | Rollen werden in einer Sitzung mit IT freigegeben. |

Gesamtdauer: ~3–4 Monate. Jede Phase einzeln rückgängig machbar.

## Daten — was bleibt, was kommt dazu

- **MySQL bleibt unverändert.** Schema, Inhalte, Backup-Vorgehen, Speicherort identisch.
- **MySQL bleibt dauerhaft Referenzsystem** — kein Abschalten geplant.
- **Lance enthält dieselben Daten** plus pro Datensatz einen 16.000-dimensionalen Fingerabdruck (für Ähnlichkeitssuche).
- **Reconciler** vergleicht MySQL und Lance laufend; Abweichung blockiert Phasenfortschritt.
- **Keine Daten verlassen die Praxis** ohne explizite Konfiguration.
- **Optionaler Wissensaustausch zwischen Praxen** ist anonymisiert (Muster, keine Daten) und standardmäßig aus.
- **Audit-Log** erfasst ab F1 jeden Lese- und Schreibvorgang.

## Was sich nicht ändert

- Desktop-App-Oberfläche
- MySQL-Schema
- Backup-Vorgehen
- Login-Verfahren
- DSGVO-Modell (nur strenger durchgesetzt)
- Keine Diagnose wird automatisch übernommen — immer Arzt-Entscheidung
- Kein Datentransfer nach außen ohne Konfiguration

## Was die Praxis tun muss

| Zeitpunkt | Aufgabe | Personalaufwand |
|---|---|---|
| Vor F1 | Freigabe Dual-Write aktivieren | – |
| Vor F3 | Freigabe Umschaltzeitfenster | – |
| Während F4 | Schulung Praxispersonal (neue Menüpunkte) | ~1 h |
| Während F5 | Rollenfreigabe mit IT | ~2 h |

Gesamt: ~3 h über 3–4 Monate, plus Standard-Backup-Verifikation.

Got the architecture grounding I needed. Key findings from `FormatBestPractices.md`:

- **CAM-PQ** = Product Quantization, designed for 1M+ document scale (32-byte codes; 10M docs in 320 MB)
- **SPO triples** = canonical persisted form (graph edges); bundled via Vsa16kF32 only at hot-path (N=3–8)
- **bgz17 palette edge = 3 bytes/edge** (way smaller than Binary16K for graph storage)
- **For < 100 items**: Vsa16kF32 fits L3, no compression needed
- **For medcare-scale (thousands of patients/praxis)**: SPO + ontology + graph nodes/edges is the natural shape; CAM-PQ only kicks in at **cross-praxis cohort scale**

Two PRs in the same week, in the same workspace, with different integration philosophies. That's not rigorous staging — that's two sessions writing locally-consistent work that isn't globally consistent.

**Things every reviewer missed:**

- **Self-merge cadence on #76/#77 not verified** — only #318 was timed (15 min). Coordinator carried the smell forward as "self-merge cadence" plural without evidence for the medcare PRs. One data point ≠ a pattern.

**Worst-case production scenario:**

F2-B wires `RlsPolicyRegistry` (row filter) but the column-mask rewriter (consuming #77's registry) gets registered separately and someone forgets the second registration. Result: doctor sees only own praxis rows ✓ but PII columns are visible to all roles ✗. Row-level audit reports no anomaly. **Partial deployment of registries is MORE dangerous than no deployment** because it creates apparent compliance without enforcement.

**Verdict: B (one grade below coordinator).** The "rigorously staged" claim is the load-bearing assertion to attack. Two parallel registries with different upstream-consumption philosophies in the same week isn't rigorous — it's parallel local consistency without global consistency. The 13-entry HIPAA registry missing birthdate + insurance is a coverage gap, not a deferral. The arc holds in intent and breaks in evidence.

Acknowledged. The principle is "**lance-graph isn't optional — it's the contract that lets external + internal ontology run as one binary.**" Concretely:

## What the framing implies

| Today | Should be |
|---|---|
| `medcare-analytics/Cargo.toml`: `default = []`; lance-graph is optional behind `lance-phase2` | `default = ["lance-phase2"]` (or just unconditional deps) |
| Modules `graph_contract / graph_engine / ontology_dto / soa_mapping / mysql_reconciler / column_mask_bridge / rls_policies / sensitive_columns` all `#[cfg(feature = "lance-phase2…")]`-gated | Unconditional `pub mod` — the contract surface IS the seam, dead-code-elim handles the rest |
| Workspace comment: "lean fallback on `claude/scaffold-medcare-rs-rZD5A` enables only the contract" | The lean fallback is a **debug-only variant**, not the canonical shape. Default is full integration. |
| `vendor/lance-graph` symlink treated as opt-in | Mandatory. Anyone building medcare-rs needs both sibling repos cloned. CI #88 enforces this. |
| Same pattern for `medcare-server`, `medcare-db` | Same flip |
| smb-office-rs equivalent (`smb-bridge` features `mongo` / `lance` / `auth-rls` opt-in) | Same architectural call applies — both halves at the hip via the contract |

## Why the 12 MB observation matters

The "lean vs full" dichotomy was justified when the contract was the only safe seam and full lance-graph was perceived as a heavy add. At 12 MB total the size argument doesn't hold — and **the AGI affinity (cognitive_shader_driver consuming BindSpace SoA, the Σ-propagation kernel, FreeEnergy gates) only delivers value if it's wired at runtime, not behind a cfg.** Headless = no AGI = same architecture as before, defeats the point.

## Concrete refactor (one PR per repo, surgical)

**medcare-rs (`claude/lance-graph-default-on`):**
1. `crates/medcare-analytics/Cargo.toml`: change `default = []` → `default = ["lance-phase2-rbac"]`. Remove `optional = true` from the three lance-graph workspace deps.
2. `crates/medcare-analytics/src/lib.rs`: remove all `#[cfg(feature = "lance-phase2")]` and `#[cfg(feature = "lance-phase2-rbac")]` gates around module declarations. Promote modules to unconditional `pub mod`.
3. Same flip in `medcare-db/Cargo.toml`, `medcare-server/Cargo.toml`.
4. Update workspace `Cargo.toml` comment block: drop the "lean fallback" framing; explicitly state both `vendor/lance-graph` and `../ndarray` are mandatory siblings.
5. Update `docs/FUTURE_STACK_ADMIN.md` §3 feature-flag-graph to reflect the new default.

**smb-office-rs (`claude/lance-graph-default-on`):**
1. `crates/smb-bridge/Cargo.toml`: change `default = []` → `default = ["mongo", "lance", "auth-rls"]`.
2. Re-export the public surface unconditionally from `lib.rs`.
3. Same workspace Cargo.toml + docs update.

**lance-graph: no change required** (no consumer-side feature flips needed; the engine has always been default-on for its own consumers).

## Risk acknowledgement

Three things this changes that someone might object to:

1. **Dev-env onboarding cost goes up.** A new contributor must clone `lance-graph` and `ndarray` as siblings before `cargo build` works. Document in `CLAUDE.md` + a `dev-setup.sh` script.
2. **Build time goes up.** Full lance-graph compile is several minutes. The `Swatinem/rust-cache` action mitigates on CI; locally first build is the cost.
3. **The current "scaffold" branch becomes orphaned.** `claude/scaffold-medcare-rs-rZD5A` was the lean-fallback canonical — it's now a historical reference. Either archive or tag it explicitly as `lean-fallback-snapshot`.

Through the `vendor/lance-graph -> ../../lance-graph` symlink, cargo's literal walk lands at `MedCare-rs/vendor/ndarray/` — not the canonical sibling `../ndarray`. Adding `vendor/ndarray -> ../../ndarray` (committed as git symlink, mode 120000) closes that gap.

**2. `cargo fmt --all` → `cargo fmt -p <each-crate>`**

`--all` recurses into every path-dep. Both ndarray fork and lance-graph workspace have rustfmt.toml with nightly features (warnings + drift on stable rustfmt). medcare-rs owns 5 crates; format those explicitly, leave upstream repos to their own CI.

**3. 53-file fmt sweep**

Applying `cargo fmt -p medcare-core -p medcare-db -p medcare-analytics -p medcare-pdf -p medcare-server` to the workspace surfaces accumulated drift — mostly multi-line `use` statements that stable rustfmt collapses to single-line, plus a handful of line-length wraps. Pure mechanical reformat, no semantic changes.
